page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c2d server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate a strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% at 2924.48. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, scoring 5863.06 in stress-ng multi-core and 3743.0 in Geekbench multi-core. Memory performance is mixed; while large block read-only and write memory bandwidths are average, the Passmark memory score is poor, falling into the bottom 10%. Single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and text processing show average performance, whereas multi-threaded operations like bzip3 decompression, software compilation, and ray tracing are weak.

Given its compute-focused, low-RAM configuration and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs, combined with weak multi-core scaling, makes it less suitable for heavy database operations, large-scale parallel processing, or machine learning training. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and small-scale utility tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores and 8.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU capabilities, though multi-core scaling and memory performance are generally weak. Large block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels, while database operations and software compilation show weaker results. Given its hardware profile, the c2d-highcpu-4 is a cost-effective option for compute-bound workloads with low memory requirements. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight web serving, and basic text processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance for lightweight compute workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized instance with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, suited for single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a low memory footprint for single-threaded processing tasks.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Configured with 8.0 GB of RAM and no local storage
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Exhibits weak multi-core scaling and low memory performance
Well-suited for single-threaded applications and lightweight web serving
